title: CSS Text and Fonts
description: Master CSS text styling and typography. Learn to control font families, sizes, weights, text alignment, decoration, and spacing to create readable and visually appealing text content.
learningObjectives: Apply font families and fallback fonts
Control font size, weight, and style
Align text and control line height
Manage text spacing and letter spacing

subtopics.title: CSS Font Families
subtopics.content:
The font-family property specifies the font for an element. It can hold multiple font names as a fallback system.

**Generic Font Families:**
- `serif` - Times New Roman, Georgia
- `sans-serif` - Arial, Helvetica, Verdana
- `monospace` - Courier New, monospace
- `cursive` - Comic Sans, cursive fonts
- `fantasy` - Impact, fantasy fonts

**Specific Font Families:**
```css
p {
  font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
}
```

**Font Stack (Fallback System):**
```css
body {
  font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif;
}
```
- Browser tries first font
- If not available, tries next
- Falls back to generic family

**Best Practices:**
- Always end with a generic font family
- Use quotes for font names with spaces
- Test fonts across different operating systems
- Consider web fonts for custom typography

glossary.definition: The font or set of fonts used to display text.
A list of font families with fallback options if a font is not available.
The thickness or darkness of characters in a font.
The vertical space between lines of text.
Visual effects added to text (underline, line-through, etc.).
Controls capitalization of text (uppercase, lowercase, capitalize).
Adds shadow effects to text for depth and emphasis.
Controls how whitespace inside elements is handled.
A general category of fonts (serif, sans-serif, monospace, etc.).
